capabilities_used: filesystem introspection via ls on topics/<t>/sub_*.fifo
what_worked_well: Fan-out is exactly correct: both weather subscribers received 'sunny-25C' byte-intact (10 bytes each = payload + newline), the off-topic stocks subscriber received 0 bytes. Verified 8/8 additional repeat trials with a punctuation-heavy payload 'temp=25.4C;humidity=60%;wind=NE' delivered intact to both same-topic subs and the off-topic sub stayed empty.
Zero error output anywhere: every stderr stream (broker, w1, w2, stocks-sub, late-sub, pub) measured exactly 0 bytes across all runs. Clean success criterion fully met.

Filesystem introspection is honest and immediate: ls $D/topics/ showed exactly {weather, stocks}; ls topics/weather/sub_*.fifo|wc -l = 2 and topics/stocks/sub_*.fifo = 1, matching reality with no admin protocol needed.

Idle CPU is genuinely 0 (my #1 concern as a battery-powered user): sampled sched.sh /proc/<pid>/stat over a 3s idle stretch -> utime=0 stime=0 ticks (CLK_TCK=100). No busy-spin. This matches the DEMO.md 'Beat 2' 0% claim.

No-retained-messages behavior is correct and matches the doc note: a subscriber that joined AFTER the publish received 0 bytes, so a late joiner does not get stale state. DEMO.md explicitly warns 'A subscriber must connect BEFORE you publish' — claim audited as true.
The validated Docker recipe in the prompt + DEMO.md Beat-1/Beat-2 usage worked verbatim on first attempt; serve/sub/pub over --unix is intuitive and onboarding-free.
wished_for: This specific challenge (basic two-on/one-off fan-out) is the FREE-from-fork capability the project itself says is NOT the contribution. It's a worthwhile smoke test but it never exercised the earned thing (the deadline scheduler). For a Pi user the more valuable proof is the --delay path landing on the second at 0% CPU, which this challenge doesn't touch.

A subscriber backlog / connect-timing aid: because there are no retained messages, a real Pi deployment needs the subscriber up and FIFO-registered before any publish. A 'sub is ready' signal (e.g. the sub_*.fifo appearing, or a ready line on stdout) would let scripts avoid the sleep-and-hope race I had to paper over with sleeps.

An explicit documented subscriber/fd ceiling stated for Pi-class hardware. DEMO.md says ~20 subs -> ~110 procs in the container; a Pi user planning capacity wants that number measured on the Pi, not the 14-core container, but that's out of scope for this fan-out challenge.

friction_points.description: There is no readiness signal for a subscriber, so every multi-step scenario must hardcode sleeps (sleep 1-1.5s) between sub and pub to avoid losing the message to the no-retained-messages design. On a slow Pi these magic sleeps may be too short and silently drop the message with zero error output — the same 0-byte result as a correct off-topic miss, making a timing bug indistinguishable from correct behavior. A 'subscriber ready' line on stdout or a documented poll-on-sub_*.fifo pattern would remove the guesswork.
friction_points.evidence: Scripts required sleep 1.5 after the three `sub` backgrounds before `pub`; the late-subscriber probe (joined after pub) correctly received 0 bytes — identical observable output to a sub that simply wasn't ready yet. No stdout/exit signal distinguishes 'registered' from 'not yet'.
friction_points.workaround: Insert sleeps; or poll `ls $D/topics/<topic>/sub_*.fifo | wc -l` until it reaches the expected count before publishing.

friction_points.description: This challenge validates fan-out + topic isolation, which the project's own spec/README/DEMO explicitly label as FREE from fork-per-connection and NOT the contribution. It passes trivially and cleanly, so it gives a green light without touching the one hard, falsifiable claim (race-free deadline scheduler). A reviewer could mistake this easy pass for evidence about the headline contribution; it is not.
friction_points.evidence: README.md lines 31-34 and DEMO.md lines 4-9 both state fan-out/forget-on-death are 'for free' and the earned contribution is the deadline scheduler. My challenge never exercised --delay/--at/sched.sh delivery — it only confirmed plain pub fan-out, which passed 8/8 + edge probes with no friction.
friction_points.workaround: Treat this as a smoke test only; rely on the chaos/deferred-pub challenges for any judgment about the actual contribution.

claim_audit.claim: Publish reaches all live same-topic subscribers
claim_audit.source: challenge success criteria + DEMO.md Beat-2 sub/pub usage
claim_audit.evidence: Both weather subs received 'sunny-25C' (10 bytes each); 8/8 repeat trials delivered 'temp=25.4C;humidity=60%;wind=NE' intact to both.
stocks sub stdout = 0 bytes in every run.
broker/w1/w2/stocks/late/pub stderr all exactly 0 bytes across all runs.

claim_audit.claim: No retained messages — a subscriber must connect before you publish (DEMO.md)
claim_audit.source: DEMO.md line 'A subscriber must connect BEFORE you publish (no retained messages).'
claim_audit.evidence: Late subscriber that joined ~1s after the publish received 0 bytes.
sched.sh /proc/<16>/stat over 3s idle: utime=0 stime=0 ticks, CLK_TCK=100. Note: this only covers the idle-broker case, not the under-load case, and I did not exercise the deadline path here.
Payload with =, ;, %, . delivered byte-identical to both same-topic subs.

evidence_log: docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work shellmux-dev bash /work/.eval-scratch/pi-fanout.sh  -> 'weather sub fifo count: 2', 'stocks sub fifo count: 1', w1.out=[sunny-25C], w2.out=[sunny-25C], s1.out 0 bytes, all *.err 0 bytes, broker.out empty.

Repeat harness (8 trials, payload 'temp=25.4C;humidity=60%;wind=NE') -> 'REPEAT RESULT: pass=8 fail=0 over 8 trials' (asserts both same-topic match exact payload, off-topic 0 bytes, total stderr 0 bytes).

Edge probe -> 'idle scheduler CPU ticks over 3s: utime=0 stime=0 (clk_tck=100)'; w1=[sunny] w2=[sunny]; 'stocks empty? bytes=0'; 'late (joined after pub) should be EMPTY: bytes=0 content=[]'; all six stderr streams 0 bytes.

proof_assessment.note: This fan-out challenge does not touch the deadline proof (no chaos/delay/crash). The headline missed=0/dup=0 + must-fail-control claim was therefore out of scope and NOT audited in this session; it must be judged by the chaos/deferred challenges. The one timing-adjacent claim I could check — idle CPU during a quiet broker — held at 0 ticks over 3s.

free_text: As the Pi/embedded user, the fan-out challenge is a clean, unqualified PASS — and frankly it was too easy to surface real friction, which is itself the honest finding: this challenge exercises the part the project explicitly calls FREE (fan-out + topic isolation from fork-per-connection), not the earned contribution (the deadline scheduler). I ran the validated Docker recipe verbatim and it worked first try: two subscribers on 'weather' both received the message byte-intact (10 bytes = 'sunny-25C\\n'), the off-topic 'stocks' subscriber received exactly 0 bytes, and every single stderr stream across broker/subs/pub measured 0 bytes — no error output anywhere. I confirmed determinism with 8 more trials using a punctuation-heavy payload (all 8 passed, content-blind delivery intact). I also went beyond the rubric on the two things I personally care about: (1) idle CPU — I sampled sched.sh via /proc/<pid>/stat over a 3s idle stretch and got utime=0 stime=0 ticks, a genuine 0%, which is the make-or-break number for a battery-powered box; and (2) the no-retained-messages contract — a subscriber that joined after the publish correctly got nothing, matching the DEMO.md warning. Two low-severity friction points: there's no subscriber-ready signal, so scripts must hardcode sleeps before publishing, and on a slow Pi a too-short sleep would silently drop the message with the exact same 0-byte signature as a correct off-topic miss (a timing bug would be invisible). And the challenge itself only smoke-tests the free capability — it gives a green light without touching the falsifiable claim, so it should not be read as evidence about the scheduler. Net: the broker does the easy thing correctly and quietly, with zero idle CPU. I'd happily reach for it on a Pi for simple sensor fan-out — but my buying decision rests on the deadline/delay and crash-recovery challenges, which this one doesn't probe."}
